prompt:
Goal: Check whether `shellcrashgo` has finished rewriting the **core features** of `ref/ShellCrash-dev`. If a core feature is missing or different, fix it in Go. When all core features are either implemented or clearly marked as intentionally not implemented, stop.

Rules (always):
- Follow `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`: only do **file/logic level** work. Use temporary directories. Do not touch iptables/nftables/routes/DNS/system proxy or start real network daemons on this machine.

Per iteration (one Ralph run = one feature):
- Use your own judgment to look at `ref/ShellCrash-dev` and identify **one core feature or flow** (for example: main start/stop flow, key config generation, subscription conversion, important protocol/mode, etc.).
- Create or update `docs/core_parity_progress.md`:
  - Add an entry for this feature with:
    - Short name and description
    - Where it lives in `ref/ShellCrash-dev`
    - Where it lives in `shellcrashgo` (or note that it is missing / intentionally not implemented)
    - Status: `done`, `missing`, `partial`, or `intentionally not implemented`
- If status is `missing` or `partial` and the feature should exist in Go:
  - Change `shellcrashgo` code/tests so this feature matches the reference behavior as closely as reasonable, using only temp dirs and file-level checks (no real network changes).
  - From `shellcrashgo` directory, run:
    - `go build ./...`
    - `go test ./...`
  - Update the feature’s status in `docs/core_parity_progress.md` to the new correct value and add brief notes.
- If the feature is intentionally not implemented in Go:
  - Set status to `intentionally not implemented` and write a short reason in `docs/core_parity_progress.md`.

Completion for this whole task:
- `docs/core_parity_progress.md` exists.
- Every **core** feature you can reasonably identify in `ref/ShellCrash-dev` has an entry with status **not** `missing`/`partial` (only `done` or `intentionally not implemented`).
- `shellcrashgo` builds and its tests pass: `cd shellcrashgo && go build ./... && go test ./...`.
- All work respected the no-real-network rule above.

Only when all completion conditions are true, output: COMPLETE
